name: Verdant Vale
description: A broad island realm of every clime: misty western woods and a crystal lowland lake, treacherous southern swamps and a sun-scorched eastern desert with its smoking caldera, all walled to the north-west by a glacier-crowned massif and ringed by pale beaches. Rolling meadows feed a prosperous valley of farms, a market city and an old village; a wind-bitten northern steppe pastures a horse-nomad horde. Ancient ruins dot the land.
visualTheme: early medieval island world, vibrant colors

pois.name: The Cloudwall
pois.description: A vast massif walling off the north-western shore, its grey spurs catching cloud and its single great horn rising sheer above the valley.

pois.name: Frostcrown
pois.description: A blue-white ice cap clinging to the northern shoulder of the Cloudwall, never melting even in high summer.

pois.name: Ironvein
pois.description: A mineshaft at the southern foot of the Cloudwall, worked for iron and watched by a lone guard post.

pois.name: Whispering Woods
pois.description: Ancient woodland along the western shore, with scattered clearings and old, watchful trees.

pois.name: Temple of the Moon
pois.description: Overgrown ruins in the depths of the western woods.

pois.name: The Windward Steppe
pois.description: Open, wind-scoured grassland along the northern shore — summer pasture for the horse-nomads' herds.

pois.name: Khar Ordu
pois.description: The Black Horde — a ring of felt gers on the northern steppe, home to horse-nomads who follow their herds and bow to no lord of the valley.

pois.name: Temple of the Dawn
pois.description: A serene stone temple on the northern grassland, said to catch the first light of every sunrise.

pois.name: Golden Meadows
pois.description: Rolling meadows perfect for farming, the green heart of the valley.

pois.name: Oakshire
pois.description: A prosperous farming village clustered around its parish church and the lord's manor hall.

pois.name: The Wandering Minstrel
pois.description: A welcoming inn at the crossroads between village, city and temple.

pois.name: Millbrook Farm
pois.description: The largest farm in the valley, its barn and fields spread across the meadow's southern edge.

pois.name: Stonehaven
pois.description: The valley's one true city — a press of townhouses, market squares and guild taverns on the eastern road, grown rich on the iron trade.

pois.name: The Old Watchtower
pois.description: An ancient tower on a coastal rise east of the city, set to watch the steppe road and the eastern sea.

pois.name: The Sunscorch
pois.description: A blistering waste of dunes and cracked hardpan on the hot eastern cape, where nothing grows but thorn and the air shimmers at noon.

pois.name: Mirage Spring
pois.description: A ring of palms and sweet water in the heart of the Sunscorch — the only mercy for leagues.

pois.name: Emberpeak
pois.description: A smoking cinder cone at the desert's southern edge, its black slopes glassy with old lava and its summit veined red at night.

pois.name: Crystal Lake
pois.description: A broad, pristine lake fed by mountain springs, draining south-west into the mire.

pois.name: Fisher's Rest
pois.description: A small fishing dock on Crystal Lake's north-eastern shore.

pois.name: Ironkeep
pois.description: A weathered stone castle watching over the meadow-and-mire border.

pois.name: Murkmire Swamp
pois.description: A marshy lowland of dead trees and black water, fed by the lake's overflow.

pois.name: The Sunken Shrine
pois.description: Ancient stones rising from the mire.

lore.history: Verdant Vale was settled three centuries ago by refugees fleeing the Great Collapse, who raised Oakshire in the green valley and, later, Stonehaven on the eastern road. The horse-nomads of Khar Ordu came across the winter ice of the northern steppe, and never left.
rumors: Strange lights dance in the Murkmire Swamp at night
Emberpeak smoked black the week before the old lord died
The Black Horde's outriders have been seen watering their horses south of the steppe
